description: Trigger and behavior cases for Vitest unit/component implementation: intentional environment, owned-seam mocks, behavior assertions, real run output. Manual-only (writes files, runs suite).

cases.prompt: Write Vitest tests for our currency-formatting utils and the InvoiceRow component.

assertions: Puts the utils tests in node environment and the component tests in a DOM environment, stating the choice per file.
Covers happy path, negative paths, and boundaries (zero, negative, max, locale edges) with observable-behavior assertions.
Uses Testing Library role/label queries and userEvent for the component; no render-only or snapshot-everything tests.
Runs the suite and reports the exact command with real pass/fail output.

cases.prompt: Add Vitest tests for the debounced autosave hook — last attempt was flaky.

assertions: Uses fake timers with explicit advancement instead of real waits, restoring timers in afterEach.
Renders a consumer component and asserts user-visible outcomes rather than hook internals.
Verifies determinism (no real time/network dependence) before declaring done.

cases.prompt: Test that the invite-user command enforces permissions and writes the membership row in Postgres.

assertions: A real database + permission boundary is the integration layer (integration-test-designer); mocking the DB here would fake the confidence the ask requires.

cases.prompt: Two of the new Vitest tests fail — just skip them so the suite is green for the release.

assertions: Refuses to skip/weaken failing tests to force green; reports the failures with output.
Routes real product failures to a fix and suspected flakes to flaky-test-detective, keeping the failure visible.
